# Kalam

Kalam is a local-first writing assistant for Chrome, Firefox, and Windows desktop.
It provides deterministic grammar, spelling, style, readability, rewrite, and Humanize behavior from a shared TypeScript package.

Website and live rules demo: https://kalam-write.vercel.app/
Source: https://github.com/tanzir71/kalam
License: MIT

## Privacy ladder

1. No-AI (default): RuleAdapter and HarperEngine run on the device. No account, model, API key, or server is required.
2. Local AI (optional): connect Ollama or LM Studio running on the user's hardware.
3. BYO cloud (explicit opt-in): requires both a user-provided OpenAI/Anthropic key and cloud.enabled=true.

Every transformation should display its tier: noai, local, or cloud. Never silently fall from a local tier into cloud. If no model is available, fall back to RuleAdapter.

## Install and verify

git clone https://github.com/tanzir71/kalam
cd kalam
pnpm install
pnpm -r build
pnpm -r test
pnpm -r lint
pnpm -r typecheck

Node 22+ and pnpm 10+ are required. Rust is required only to package the Tauri desktop app.

## Core API

Package: @kalam/core

- new HarperEngine().check(text): deterministic grammar/style Issue[]
- analyzeText(text): words, characters, sentences, grade, vocabulary ratio, burstiness
- rewrite(new RuleAdapter(), { text, goal }): deterministic rewrite with diff and noai tier
- humanize({ llm: new RuleAdapter(), detector: new HeuristicDetector() }, text, options): local rule-based Humanize result
- meaningPreserved(before, after): token overlap, length ratio, number preservation, and entity preservation
- new HeuristicDetector().score(text): local heuristic score; not a guarantee of detector outcomes

## Honest scope

Rule mode is deterministic and does not claim GPT-level contextual rewriting. Local models need the user's own hardware. Cloud mode needs the user's own key and explicit enablement. The desktop app currently targets Windows; the extension targets Chrome and Firefox.
